c74object.name: tap.sustain~
c74object.digest: Capture recent audio into seamless sustaining loops
c74object.description: Continuously records its signal input. On a bang the most recently recorded material is captured into a voice, trimmed to significant-power zero-crossings at both ends, and played back as a crossfaded forward/backward loop so it sustains without clicks. A bank of voices is allocated round-robin so successive bangs stack overlapping loops, each rising in over the rise time.

method.name: clear
method.description: Erase all captured loops (every voice) and reset the round-robin allocation cursor.

method.name: bang
method.description: Capture the most recently recorded audio into a sustaining loop, allocated into the next voice round-robin so it overlaps any voices already sounding.

attribute.name: fade
attribute.digest: Length of crossfade across loop-points in milliseconds
attribute.description: Length of crossfade across loop-points in milliseconds.

attribute.name: length
attribute.digest: Maximum length of the captured loop in milliseconds
attribute.description: Maximum length of the captured loop in milliseconds. Changing this restarts the recording buffer.

attribute.name: rise
attribute.description: Time over which each newly captured voice grows to full power, in milliseconds. Applied independently per voice as a one-shot fade-in when it starts sustaining, so a stacked voice swells in under the others rather than clicking in.

attribute.name: voices
attribute.description: Number of overlapping sustaining voices (1-5, default 5). Each bang captures into the next voice round-robin, so up to this many loops can stack before the oldest is recycled.
